title: Rock Splitters, Quarry
description: Separate blocks of rough dimension stone from quarry mass using jackhammers, wedges, or chop saws.

tasks.text: Locate grain line patterns to determine how rocks will split when cut.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove pieces of stone from larger masses, using jackhammers, wedges, and other tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert wedges and feathers into holes, and drive wedges with sledgehammers to split stone sections from masses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark dimensions or outlines on stone prior to cutting, using rules and chalk lines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut slabs of stone into sheets that will be used for floors or counters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set charges of explosives to split rock.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drill holes along outlines, using jackhammers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drill holes into sides of stones broken from masses, insert dogs or attach slings, and direct removal of stones.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Cut grooves along outlines, using chisels.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
